incidents.description: Ninth wave; IRGC claims 700+ drones and hundreds of missiles launched since operation began. Cluster munition warhead used against Tel Aviv area (12 injuries). Deadliest strike of the war: Emad ballistic missile struck residential area in Beit Shemesh, destroying a synagogue and collapsing a public bomb shelter beneath it. Nine killed including three teenage siblings (Yaakov, 16; Avigail, 15; Sara Biton, 13), Ronit Elimelech and her mother Sara, mother and son Bruria and Yosef Cohen, Gavriel Baruch Ravach, and Oren Katz. 28 wounded including two in serious condition. Hospitals in Jerusalem treated 50 people from the Beit Shemesh impact.

impact.damage: Missile fragments and submunitions from cluster bomb warhead impacted several areas in central Israel, causing damage. One impact site in a central town sparked a fire. Police confirmed cluster bomb warhead. Each submunition carries ~2.5 kg explosive, ~20 submunitions per warhead, dispersal radius ~8 km. Submunitions designed to explode on impact; unexploded submunitions pose landmine-like hazard.

Eighth Iranian attack since midnight. Sirens sounded in Tel Aviv and surrounding area. Police confirmed cluster bomb warhead; missile fragments and submunitions impacted several areas. One impact in a central town sparked a fire. No immediate injuries reported. IDF Home Front Command cleared shelters after attack. Iran has fired at least six cluster missiles at Israel since the war began per Haaretz reporting.

idf.statement: IDF Home Front Command says civilians in areas where sirens sounded can leave bomb shelters but should remain close. No immediate injuries reported.

incidents.description: Twenty-first wave of Operation True Promise 4. Codename 'Ya Mu'izz al-Mu'minin.' IRGC Aerospace Force launched combined suicide drone swarms and Kheibar Shekan ballistic missiles with cluster warheads at Tel Aviv early Friday morning on Day 7 of the conflict. IRGC stated the tactic was designed to overwhelm Israel's multi-layered air defences. Iran vowed 'more intense' operations in coming days. Cumulative munitions prior to this wave reportedly exceeded 2,000 drones and 600 missiles.

incidents.description: Twenty-second wave of Operation True Promise 4. Codename 'Ya Hossein ibn Ali.' Significant escalation in weapon sophistication: first wave to combine Khorramshahr-4 (~1,500 kg warhead, Mach 16), Kheibar Shekan, and Fattah hypersonic missiles simultaneously. Targeted Tel Aviv, Ben Gurion Airport, Haifa military centers, plus Al Udeid and Al Dhafra air bases in the Gulf. IRGC framed as retaliation for Minab school strike. Iranian state media stated most missiles used in the war to date were from 2012-2014 production, and new generation weapons had only been deployed in rare cases until this wave.

idf.statement: Israeli military confirmed defence systems were intercepting missiles launched from Iran. No injuries reported.

us_government.statement: US says Iran's ballistic missile attacks have fallen by 90 percent since the first day of the conflict; drone attacks down 83 percent.

incidents.iranian_media_claims: IRGC Public Relations announced the 22nd wave of Operation True Promise 4 began with a barrage of Khorramshahr 4, Kheibar, and Fattah missiles targeting the heart of the Israeli-occupied territories. The operation was codenamed 'Ya Hossein ibn Ali' and conducted in retaliation for the child-killing perpetrators who murdered Iran's children at the Minab school. IRGC stated only a fraction of Iran's actual capabilities had been used and new generation strategic weapons were forthcoming.

incidents.description: Forty-second wave of Operation True Promise 4. Day 13 (March 12), overnight into Wednesday. IRGC announced its 'most intense and heaviest operation' since the start of the war. Multiple ballistic missile salvos fired at Israel throughout the night, triggering sirens in central Israel at approximately 11:30 PM (March 11), 12:50 AM, 3:40 AM, and 4:40 AM (March 12). Coordinated with Hezbollah, which launched approximately 150 rockets at northern Israel. Khorramshahr-4 heavy ballistic missiles confirmed alongside Fattah hypersonic and Kheibar Shekan missiles. Cluster munition warheads deployed; two construction workers killed in Yehud (east of Tel Aviv) by submunitions from a cluster-equipped missile that penetrated defences. Iran also targeted US bases in Kuwait and Bahrain and struck at Gulf state targets including Qatar, Saudi Arabia, and the UAE. Israeli media reported all missiles intercepted, though cluster submunitions caused casualties on the ground.

timing.announced_utc_note: First salvo at 11:30 PM Israel time (March 11 local). Subsequent salvos at 12:50 AM, 3:40 AM, and 4:40 AM March 12 local. Per Haaretz and Times of Israel liveblog reporting.

interception.interception_report: Israeli media reported all ballistic missiles targeting Israel were intercepted. However, cluster submunitions from at least one missile dispersed over the Yehud area east of Tel Aviv, killing two and injuring one. Saudi Arabia intercepted 6 missiles near Prince Sultan Air Base. Kuwait shot down 8 drones. UAE intercepted drones and missiles. Bahrain activated air raid sirens.

Two construction workers killed outdoors (not in shelters) — one killed immediately, second died the following day. One worker seriously injured by shrapnel. Dedicated to the memory of Hossein Salami and martyrs of previous campaigns. Continues the pattern of 6 salvos on March 12; sirens sounded across central Israel. No casualties reported from daytime attacks. A teenage girl in Rehovot was fatally struck by a vehicle while rushing to shelter during one of the day's attacks (sirens did not go off in her city).

incidents.description: Forty-eighth wave of Operation True Promise 4. Day 15 (March 14). IRGC launched solid-fuel Kheibar Shekan, liquid-fuel Qadr missiles, and attack drones targeting Israeli military sites in the Galilee, occupied Golan Heights, and Haifa, plus US military bases. Multiple salvos throughout the day (at least 5 since midnight). Major impact event: missile with fragmenting/cluster warhead struck Eilat at ~3:43 PM Israel time, causing ten impact sites — 12-year-old boy moderately injured by glass shards, 39-year-old man and one other person lightly injured by shrapnel. Central Israel: fragmenting missile hit Lod/Ness Ziona area with no injuries. Jerusalem targeted with interceptions, no casualties. Western Galilee sirens for suspected Hezbollah drone infiltration at Nahariya. Hezbollah claimed targeting settlements in Gronot HaGalil and Israeli air defense system in Ma'alot-Tarshiha. Coordinated with Hezbollah.

incidents.description: Sixtieth wave of Operation True Promise 4. Day 17 (March 16). Afternoon missile barrage targeting central Israel and Jerusalem area. Shrapnel from interceptions fell near the Knesset, the National Library, and the Church of the Holy Sepulchre in Jerusalem. A large intercepted missile chunk damaged a home in East Jerusalem. Israel Fire and Rescue Authority reported 10 locations damaged by intercepted missile shrapnel or cluster munitions — seven in Rishon Lezion, with additional impacts in Lod, Shoham, and along Highway 431. Interceptor debris fell in Safed, northern Israel, initially suspected of leaking hazardous material but later ruled out by the Environmental Protection Ministry. Day 17 aggregate: Health Ministry reported 142 injured over 24 hours — 3 in moderate condition, 134 in good condition, 5 treated for anxiety. IDF launched extensive counterstrikes on Iranian targets in Tehran, Shiraz, and Tabriz simultaneously.

incidents.description: Sixty-first wave of Operation True Promise 4. Day 17 (March 16). Combined attack targeting UAE, Qatar, Saudi Arabia, and Iraq. UAE air defenses intercepted 6 ballistic missiles and 21 drones launched from Iran. A drone attack caused a fire at the Fujairah petroleum industrial zone, forcing suspension of oil loading operations. One Palestinian civilian killed in Abu Dhabi (Al Bahyah area) when a missile struck their car. Qatar armed forces intercepted two waves of Iranian missiles over Doha. Saudi Arabia intercepted 64 drones — 61 aimed at Eastern Province and 3 over Riyadh. In Iraq, air defenses intercepted a drone near the US embassy in Baghdad; one federal police officer and two PMF members injured at Jurf al-Sakhar south of Baghdad.

timing.announced_utc_note: Approximate timing based on reporting sequence. UAE interceptions announced afternoon local time (~14:24 UTC per Gulf News timestamps). Qatar interceptions at ~12:30 and ~13:00 UTC. Saudi interceptions from morning through early afternoon.

Abu Dhabi Al Bahyah area (1 civilian killed by missile strike on car). Qatar: two waves of missiles intercepted over Doha (~12:30 and 13:00 UTC). Saudi Arabia: 64 drones intercepted (61 Eastern Province, 3 Riyadh). Iraq: drone near US embassy Baghdad intercepted; Jurf al-Sakhar attack (1 federal police officer, 2 PMF members injured).

incidents.description: Eighty-fifth salvo. Day 22 (March 21). Iranian ballistic missile with cluster bomb warhead strikes Rishon Lezion, central Israel at ~08:00 IST. Cluster submunitions hit empty daycare and at least 6 other sites. MDA confirmed 7 impact sites including 2 residential buildings. No injuries — Saturday, education facilities closed. Mayor: 'there could have been kids at this kindergarten.' Education minister subsequently cancelled Passover camps. 4th salvo since midnight. IAF reportedly conserving interceptors, choosing not to shoot down some cluster bomblets.

timing.announced_utc_note: Approximately 08:00 IST (06:00 UTC) March 21. 4th Iranian ballistic missile salvo since midnight. Cluster munition impact in Rishon Lezion. ToI liveblog.

impact.damage: Cluster munition submunitions hit empty daycare — children's toys and dolls scattered among rubble. 7 impact sites across Rishon Lezion confirmed by MDA, including damage to 2 residential buildings. incidents.description: Eighty-sixth salvo. Day 22 (March 21). Iran fires 2 intermediate-range ballistic missiles at Diego Garcia US-UK military base in Indian Ocean — ~4,000 km from Iran. First known Iranian strike at this range. One missile failed in flight; the other was targeted by SM-3 interceptor from US warship. Both unsuccessful. Iran may have used Simorgh space launch vehicle as booster to extend range beyond known 2,000 km limit. UK confirmed attack was 'unsuccessful.' Demonstrates Iranian missiles could theoretically reach Western Europe.

timing.announced_utc_note: Approximate timing. Reported March 21 by WSJ, CNBC, The Hill. Exact launch time not disclosed. Iran state media (Mehr) confirmed the strike.
timing.announcement_source: WSJ / CNBC / UK Government / Mehr News Agency

targets.targets: Diego Garcia US-UK joint military base, British Indian Ocean Territory. ~4,000 km from Iran. First known Iranian strike at intercontinental range.

launch_site.geolocation_source: estimated — Semnan area (Iran space launch facilities)

impact.damage: No damage. One missile failed in flight. Other targeted by SM-3 interceptor from US warship. UK confirmed attack unsuccessful.

escalation.coalition_response: First known use of IRBM at ~4,000 km range. Demonstrates capability to reach Western Europe. Significant escalation in range.

incidents.iranian_media_claims: Mehr News Agency and Iranian state media boasted missile range 'is beyond what the enemy previously imagined.'

incidents.description: Ninetieth salvo. Day 22 (March 21). Iranian ballistic missile direct hit on Dimona, southern Israel — interception failed. 12 impact sites across residential area. ~450 kg warhead. Building collapse. 30-51 injured including 12-year-old boy in serious condition. IRGC framed as retaliation for Natanz. IAEA confirmed no damage to Negev Nuclear Research Center. IRGC confirmed Emad and Qadr missiles used. IDF investigating interception failure.

incidents.description: Ninety-first salvo. Day 22 (March 21). Iranian ballistic missile direct hit on Arad, southern Israel — interception failed. Missile struck between three residential buildings in Gur Hasidim neighborhood. 4 buildings completely collapsed, 9 buildings sustained significant damage. Mass casualty event declared by Magen David Adom. At least 8 killed, 64-100+ injured including 5-year-old girl in serious condition. Dozens trapped in rubble. IDF investigating interception failure. Khorramshahr-4 (Kheibar) hypersonic missile with ~500 kg warhead suspected.

timing.announced_utc_note: Approximately 17:30 IST (15:30 UTC) March 21. Shortly after Dimona strike. Mass casualty event declared. ToI liveblog, JPost, multiple sources.
timing.announcement_source: IDF / Magen David Adom / Times of Israel / Jerusalem Post

impact.damage: Mass casualty event declared by Magen David Adom. Missile struck between three residential buildings in Gur Hasidim neighborhood. 4 buildings completely collapsed. 9 buildings sustained significant structural damage. At least 8 killed. 7-8 in serious/critical condition including 5-year-old girl. 14-15 in moderate condition. ~42 lightly injured. Dozens trapped in rubble. Netanyahu spoke with mayors of both Dimona and Arad.

incidents.iranian_media_claims: IRGC framed Dimona/Arad strikes as part of Wave 72 of Operation True Promise 4, targeting sites in Israel and US Fifth Fleet. Described as retaliation for Natanz.

Man in his 50s seriously wounded by shrapnel. 13 others in good condition from shrapnel/blast. Total: 1 killed, 14 injured.

incidents.description: One hundred and eighth salvo. Day 28 (March 27). IRGC Wave 82. Iranian Army targeted Haifa port with large swarm of kamikaze drones. Also missiles targeting military command center in Israel, industries near Dead Sea linked to nuclear program. Plus US targets (Arifjan Kuwait, Al-Kharj Saudi Arabia, Sheikh Isa Bahrain, Ali al-Salem Kuwait). BAZAN Group refinery in Haifa reportedly hit.
